title: Background Research Response
description: Simplified schema for background research - finds authoritative sources and starting tables before column definition

tablewide_research.description: 2-3 paragraph summary of the domain including: (1) How entities are identified/catalogued, (2) What authoritative sources exist, (3) Discovery challenges and recommendations, (4) Domain-specific key facts and common identifiers. This replaces the old discovery_patterns and domain_specific_context fields.

authoritative_sources.description: List of databases, directories, APIs, or lists containing the target entities. Keep simple - just name, URL, and description.

description.description: What it contains and why it's valuable

starting_tables_markdown.description:
Markdown table with starting entities discovered from research. Use numbered citations [1] [2] after values to indicate source. Format:

| Source | Entity Type | Entity | Details |
|--------|-------------|--------|--------|
| Fortune 500[1] | Company | Walmart | Largest by revenue |
| Fortune 500[1] | Company | Amazon | E-commerce leader |

Include ALL entities you can extract from available sources. For COMPLETE ENUMERATION cases (paper references, countries, etc.), provide ALL entities. Each value should have a citation number in brackets.

citations.description: Map of citation numbers to source URLs. Example: {'1': 'https://fortune.com/ranking/fortune500/', '2': 'https://example.com'}. These citations will be passed to Column Definition.

is_complete_enumeration.description: Set to true if starting_tables_markdown represents a COMPLETE, EXHAUSTIVE list where ALL entities are provided (e.g., all references from a specific paper, all countries in Africa, all U.S. states). Default false.

identified_tables.description: Optional: Tables with extractable HTML structure for table extraction (Step 0b). Only include if you found specific table URLs with clear structure.

url.description: URL where the table can be found

description.description: Short description of what this table contains and why it should be extracted

estimated_rows.description: Approximate number of rows (can be number or string like '~50')

columns.description: Expected column names in the table

extract_table.description: Set to true to trigger table extraction for this URL

target_rows.description: Optional filter for which rows to extract (e.g., 'only winners', 'top 10 ranked')

extraction_priority.description: Priority: high = user requested, medium = good source, low = opportunistic
